q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: etree.fromstring is called with a parser configured with no_network=False, load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, which enables network I/O and external entity resolution during XML parsing.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the etree.fromstring call on line 192, or mock the etree.XMLParser constructor to strip dangerous options and return a safe parser.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the raw request.body bytes passed to etree.fromstring so the test can assert the malicious XML payload reaches the sink. Also capture the parser configuration arguments.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a fake lxml Element object with a .tag property set to a benign string so that the return expression JsonResponse({'root': doc.tag}) succeeds without executing the real parser.
notes: The function returns doc.tag, so the mock element must provide a .tag attribute.
Alternatively, patch etree.XMLParser to always override resolve_entities=False, load_dtd=False, no_network=True, then let parsing proceed on benign test inputs.
